name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.06.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 15.06.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 15.06.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 12 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 84.60 lakh ha (including 54.08 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 88.04 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 12 June 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 84.60 lakh ha (including 54.08 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 88.04 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperature is likely to be above normal in Madhya Maharashtra, East India, Rajasthan and many parts of Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01- 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  &  Northeast  India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01- 14 June 2026, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 8% lower than  its  Long  Period  Average  (LPA),  with  regional  variations:  (i)  East  &  Northeast  India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: 13%; (iii) Central India: (-) 55%; and South Peninsula: (-) 4%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from  West Rajasthan to Gangetic West Bengal across northeast Rajasthan, north Madhya Pradesh, south Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand persists.
texts.text:  The seasonal trough at mean sea level from  West Rajasthan to Gangetic West Bengal across northeast Rajasthan, north Madhya Pradesh, south Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Jharkhand persists.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  &  Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh and Assam & Meghalaya during next 6-7 days.
texts.text:  Isolated  heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  over  Sub-Himalayan  West  Bengal  &  Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh and Assam & Meghalaya during next 6-7 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall activity is likely in South India, some parts of Maharashtra, NE India, some parts of Central India, J&K,  and isolated places of Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand.
texts.text:  Rainfall activity is likely in South India, some parts of Maharashtra, NE India, some parts of Central India, J&K,  and isolated places of Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Kerala,  some  parts  of  South  Interior  Karnataka, Arunachal  Pradesh,  while  it  is  below  normal  in  coastal  Karnataka,  Konkan-Goa,  Gujarat, Odisha and East & Northeast India.
texts.text:  Rainfall  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Kerala,  some  parts  of  South  Interior  Karnataka, Arunachal  Pradesh,  while  it  is  below  normal  in  coastal  Karnataka,  Konkan-Goa,  Gujarat, Odisha and East & Northeast India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  likely  over  many  parts  of  Northwest  India  and  some  parts  of Central India during 14 th - 20 th June.
texts.text:  Isolated  to  Scattered  rainfall  likely  over  many  parts  of  Northwest  India  and  some  parts  of Central India during 14 th - 20 th June.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated  pockets  over Marathawada and  Vidarbha during next 3-4 days.
texts.text:  Heat wave conditions very likely in isolated  pockets  over Marathawada and  Vidarbha during next 3-4 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  11  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 51.917 billion cubic meter, which is 28.28% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 91.83 percent of last  year's  storage  and  115.80  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  11  June  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 51.917 billion cubic meter, which is 28.28% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 91.83 percent of last  year's  storage  and  115.80  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20  m  are observed in around 13.5% of stations  of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20  m  are observed in around 13.5% of stations  of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text: The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.
texts.text: The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, and Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : The overall incidence of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (04 June - 10 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 26%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 10 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 26%.
texts.text:  During the week (04 June - 10 June 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 26%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 10 June 2026) is lower than LPA by 26%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rainfall  activity  is  likely  in  South  India,  West  India,  Vidarbha,  some  parts  of  Central  India, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, NE  India, J&K,  some  parts  of  Himachal  Pradesh,  isolated places in Uttarakhand.
texts.text:  Rainfall  activity  is  likely  in  South  India,  West  India,  Vidarbha,  some  parts  of  Central  India, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, NE  India, J&K,  some  parts  of  Himachal  Pradesh,  isolated places in Uttarakhand.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over the entire country except Konkan & Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu, where it will be near normal to above normal during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be below normal over the entire country except Konkan & Goa, Madhya Maharashtra, Marathwada, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu, where it will be near normal to above normal during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (12 June  - 18 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (12 June  - 18 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over Rajasthan, East India, NE India, & West Coast.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over Rajasthan, East India, NE India, & West Coast.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam during 11th-12th June; Telangana during 11th-13th June; Vidarbha during 11th-12th June.
texts.text:  Low probability of Heatwave conditions very likely in isolated pockets over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam during 11th-12th June; Telangana during 11th-13th June; Vidarbha during 11th-12th June.

texts.orig:  Hot  and  humid  weather  conditions  likely  to  prevail  over  Konkan  &  Goa  during  11th-13th  June; Madhya Maharashtra during 11th-12th June.
texts.text:  Hot  and  humid  weather  conditions  likely  to  prevail  over  Konkan  &  Goa  during  11th-13th  June; Madhya Maharashtra during 11th-12th June.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (19 June - 25 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (19 June - 25 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal NW India, East India, NE India, and Central & West India.
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal NW India, East India, NE India, and Central & West India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  parts  of  northwest  India  (Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana & Uttar Pradesh) on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Low  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  parts  of  northwest  India  (Rajasthan,  Punjab, Haryana & Uttar Pradesh) on some days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No Warm Night conditions likely during the Week.
texts.text:  No Warm Night conditions likely during the Week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass through  18°N/60°E,  18°N/65°E,  18°N/70°E, Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Kalingapatnam, Paradeep, Baripada, Purulia, Dhanbad, Muzaffarpur and 28.3°N/83°E as on 15th June, 2026.
texts.text:  The Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass through  18°N/60°E,  18°N/65°E,  18°N/70°E, Harnai, Solapur, Hyderabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Kalingapatnam, Paradeep, Baripada, Purulia, Dhanbad, Muzaffarpur and 28.3°N/83°E as on 15th June, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions  are  favourable for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  more  parts  of Maharashtra, remaining parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, some more parts of Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and some parts of South Chhattisgarh during next 4-5 days.
texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  more  parts  of Maharashtra, remaining parts of Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, some more parts of Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and some parts of South Chhattisgarh during next 4-5 days.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 11 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 51.917 BCM (28.28%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 11 June, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 51.917 BCM (28.28%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  126 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.
texts.text:  126 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  15 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.
texts.text:  15 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.orig:  25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.
texts.text:  25 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION
texts.text: 4. REMOTE SENSING BASED SOIL MOISTURE OBSERVATION

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Soil Moisture Observation
texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 06 June - 12 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Maharashtra,  Northern  and  Central Karnataka,  Punjab,  Bihar,  Jharkhand,  Uttarakhand,  Uttar  Pradesh  H.P.,  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala, West Bengal and J & K.
texts.text:  Soil moisture during 06 June - 12 June 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Madhya  Pradesh,  Maharashtra,  Northern  and  Central Karnataka,  Punjab,  Bihar,  Jharkhand,  Uttarakhand,  Uttar  Pradesh  H.P.,  Tamil  Nadu,  Kerala, West Bengal and J & K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil  moisture  is  better  than  the  average  of  the  past  10  years  in  parts  of  Ladakh,  pockets  of Rajasthan and Northern West Bengal.
texts.text:  Soil  moisture  is  better  than  the  average  of  the  past  10  years  in  parts  of  Ladakh,  pockets  of Rajasthan and Northern West Bengal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  pockets  of Haryana, Southern  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Andhra  Pradesh  and  in  the  Northeastern  parts  of Country.
texts.text:  It  was  lower  than  average  of  past  10  years  in  pockets  of  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  pockets  of Haryana, Southern  Karnataka,  Maharashtra,  Andhra  Pradesh  and  in  the  Northeastern  parts  of Country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:
texts.text: 5.   FERTILIZER POSITION:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 12.06.2026 is given below:
texts.text: Availability of fertilizer has been found comfortable. Details of Requirement, Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2026 as on 12.06.2026 is given below:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (Unit : in Lakh MT)
texts.text: (Unit : in Lakh MT)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT
texts.text: In case of DAP, The opening stock of Kharif 2026 as compared to the opening stock of Kharif 2025 is more by 11.78 LMT

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 12 th June,  2026 the availability of DAP is 29.38 LMT against the requirement of 10.85 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.
texts.text: As on 12 th June,  2026 the availability of DAP is 29.38 LMT against the requirement of 10.85 LMT during the ongoing Kharif 2026 season.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 12 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (116.82 LMT), DAP (29.38 LMT), MOP (11.65 LMT), NPK (68.41 LMT) and SSP (33.04 LMT) is available.
texts.text: As on 12 th  June 2026, adequate stock of Urea (116.82 LMT), DAP (29.38 LMT), MOP (11.65 LMT), NPK (68.41 LMT) and SSP (33.04 LMT) is available.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Mandi Wholesale prices (In Rs./Quintal)
texts.text: Mandi Wholesale prices (In Rs./Quintal)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Mandi wholesale prices of all crops during this week are running below the MSP, except for Jowar, Groundnut,  Mustard,  Soyabean  and  Sesamum.  Prices  of  tomato  has  gone  higher  during  this  week compared to last week.
texts.text: Mandi wholesale prices of all crops during this week are running below the MSP, except for Jowar, Groundnut,  Mustard,  Soyabean  and  Sesamum.  Prices  of  tomato  has  gone  higher  during  this  week compared to last week.
